summary: Comprehensive backend testing of Slabby MVP. All 20 pytest tests passed (100% backend success). Covered auth (register/login/me/auth-protection), market ticker, cards CRUD with $250 min validation and owner-only patching, listings (with sport filter), full trade lifecycle (create/accept with ownership swap + trades_completed increment + listed_for_trade auto-clear, reject), AI Fair Trade Score (Claude Sonnet 4.5 returning valid score/verdict/reasoning/values), AI Match (ranked listings), AI Comps (6 comps + average/median/summary), portfolio analytics, and user profile.

minor.issue: EmailStr/Pydantic v2 rejects .test TLD — not a backend bug per se but worth noting registration accepts only resolvable TLDs (.io, .com, etc). Minor UX consideration.
30-day history is mocked/random.seed(user_id) — value is deterministic but not real PnL. Acceptable for MVP but should be documented.
change_pct is randomly generated each call (random.uniform), so values change every request — fine for demo but not a real ticker.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~710 lines — within tolerance but trending toward the 700-line split threshold. Consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/cards.py, routers/trades.py, routers/ai.py for maintainability.
Use of @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in modern FastAPI; prefer lifespan context manager.
JWT_SECRET defaults to 'dev-secret' if env var missing — current .env sets 'slabby-dev-secret-change-me', but the fallback should fail-fast in production rather than silently use a weak secret.
POST /api/ai/comps/{card_id} is unauthenticated — anyone can hit Claude on any card. Consider gating behind current_user for cost control.
GET /api/market/ticker generates random change_pct each call without persistence — not a real market signal.
patch_card returns 404 when user is not the owner; could return 403 to be semantically correct, but this is a security-by-obscurity choice (acceptable).

Trade accept correctly swaps ownership, sets listed_for_trade=False on both sides, increments trades_completed for both users, and cancels other pending trades that reference the transferred cards — verified end-to-end.
test_credentials: demo@slabby.com / slabby123 (Alex Vance), marcus@slabby.com / slabby123 (Marcus Thorne) — both seeded; verified working.
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 2 users + 12 cards on startup. Tests created 1 TEST_ user via register and 1 TEST_ card; trade test moved 1 card between users (state changes persist in test DB but tests are order-tolerant).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_slabby_api.py. Use `pytest backend/tests/test_slabby_api.py -v`. Tests are state-tolerant: my_cards uses >=5 not ==5, listings uses >=5, because trade-accept swaps ownership and add_card adds new cards. AI tests (fair-score, match, comps) call live Claude Sonnet 4.5 via emergentintegrations — first call may take 10-30s. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in /app/backend/.env is functional.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no functional issues. Two initial test failures were test-script issues (Pydantic v2 EmailStr rejects .test TLD; tight equality assertions broken by stateful trade/add_card side effects). Both fixed in test script.
